01.title: Go2, ROS 2, Kinematics, and Visualization
01.thesis: The Unitree Go2 is a cyber-physical system where physical motion, sensor feedback, compute, network paths, and safety rules are deeply coupled.
rules: See Go2 as a system: connect body, compute, network, SDK, and safety into one working picture.
Predict data movement: learn when information is a topic stream, service request, action goal, or parameter.
Debug before control: use arrays, frames, TF, and RViz displays to verify the robot state before sending motion.

pacing.session: Lecture — Go2 System View & Kinematics
Lab 1 — ROS2 Architecture & Workspace
Practical — Go2 DDS & Low-Level Sport RPC
Lab 2 — ROS2 Services & RViz Visualization

slides.title: Why This Lecture Matters
slides.thesis: Today builds your robot mental model. The goal is not memorization; the goal is safe interpretation of robot data and software behavior.

board_data.label: 01. See Go2 as a System
board_data.value: Connect the robot body, onboard computers, network, SDK, ROS 2 graph, and safety process into one unified mental model.

board_data.label: 02. Predict Data Movement
board_data.value: Understand exactly when information should flow as a topic stream, a service request, an action goal, or a dynamic parameter.

board_data.label: 03. Debug Before Control
board_data.value: Utilize arrays, frames, TF, RViz displays, and active message inspection to verify robot state before commanding physical motors.

slides.bottom_band: Teaching Rhythm: Pause, inspect commands, and ask questions before moving to the physical hardware.

slides.title: Go2 Is More Than a Robot Body
slides.thesis: Before sending any command, ask: which layer produces the data, which layer moves it, and which layer has authority to act?

rows: Creates physical motion and physical risk. High mechanical authority.
IMU, joint state, foot force, LiDAR
Describes what the robot can observe. Essential for closed-loop control.
Separates low-level robot operation from student development work.
Decides whether software can see, monitor, or command the robot.

slides.bottom_band: Mental Model: Always see layers, not just legs.

slides.title: Go2 Platform Reasoning
slides.thesis: Why do we use quadruped platforms for research and inspection? It is a balance of physical capability and software complexity.

board_data.label: Physical Capability
board_data.value: Stairs, slopes, rough terrain, and active obstacle avoidance. Legged systems go where wheels cannot.

board_data.label: Software Complexity
board_data.value: Requires high-frequency joint control, state estimation, active balance, and dynamic gait planning.

board_data.label: Safety First
board_data.value: Unlike humanoids, quadrupeds have a stable static base. However, active gaits still present high kinetic risks.

slides.bottom_band: Platform Goal: Bridge the gap between low-level kinematics and high-level autonomous navigation.

slides.title: Go2 Onboard Computing
slides.thesis: Low-level motor control is separated from high-level user applications to protect the robot's physical stability.

rows: High-frequency joint control, balance, state estimation, safety guards.
User development, SLAM, perception, navigation, custom algorithms.
Monitoring, visualization (RViz), remote control, and build environment.

slides.bottom_band: Architecture Rule: Never run heavy compute or blocking loops on the low-level controller.

slides.title: Go2 Networking & DDS Discovery
slides.thesis: A robust local network is the nervous system of the robot. All communication relies on DDS discovery over specific interfaces.
board_data: Robot Subnet: Go2 operates on a dedicated subnet (typically 192.168.123.xx). Your workstation must join this subnet.
DDS Discovery: Data Distribution Service (DDS) allows ROS 2 nodes to discover each other automatically without a central master.
Interface Selection: You must configure 'CYCLONEDDS_URI' or 'RMW_IMPLEMENTATION' to bind to the correct physical network interface.
IP Conflict Warning: Never connect multiple workstations to the robot network with conflicting static IPs.
slides.bottom_band: Debugging Habit: Always run 'ros2 node list' or 'ros2 topic list' to verify network discovery before launching nodes.

slides.title: Go2 Safety Before Motion
slides.thesis: Safety is not a checklist; it is an active programming habit. Legged robots can fail instantly and violently.
board_data: Unsafe Pattern: Running a new motor controller node without first subscribing to the robot's High-State feedback.
Clear Workspace: Always place the robot on a metal gantry stand (suspended legs) when testing new joint commands.
Emergency Stop: Map an easily accessible key or button to send zero-torque or emergency-stop commands immediately.
Safe Recovery: Ensure your node handles exceptions gracefully and resets joint commands to neutral on exit.
slides.bottom_band: Safety Rule: Keep hands away from the joints when the robot's low-level motors are active.

slides.title: Quadruped Kinematics Naming Discipline
slides.thesis: Standardizing joint and frame naming is essential for writing clean, reusable kinematic formulas.

rows: Responsible for forward traction, steering, and obstacle clearance.
Responsible for propulsion, load-bearing, and body pitch adjustment.
Controls side-to-side leg swing. Range: -1.04 to 1.04 rad.
Controls forward/backward thigh movement. Range: -1.57 to 3.49 rad.
Controls leg extension and retraction. Range: -2.72 to -0.83 rad.

slides.bottom_band: Naming Rule: Always use the standard [Leg]_[Joint] convention in URDF and software.

slides.title: Forward Kinematics (FK) Formulation
slides.thesis: Forward Kinematics calculates the exact 3D coordinates (X, Y, Z) of the foot relative to the hip frame, given the joint angles.

steps: 1. Read joint encoder values (q_0, q_1, q_2) from the High-State message.
2. Define link lengths: Thigh link (L1 = 0.213m) and Calf link (L2 = 0.213m).
3. Apply homogeneous transformation matrices representing the leg geometry.
4. Output the 3D position of the foot center relative to the base hip frame.

slides.bottom_band: Verification: Run the 'lowstate_listener' lab to verify joint angle readings match physical leg positions.

slides.title: Inverse Kinematics (IK) Formulation
slides.thesis: Inverse Kinematics solves the inverse problem: given a target foot coordinate (X, Y, Z), what joint angles must the motors command?

steps: 1. Define the desired foot trajectory or static target relative to the hip.
2. Check if the target is within the leg's physical workspace envelope.
3. Solve trigonometric or geometric equations to find q_roll, q_pitch, q_knee.
4. Guard against imaginary numbers: an unreachable target produces math errors!

slides.bottom_band: Debugging Habit: Always clamp IK outputs to physical joint limits before sending them to the motors.

slides.title: ROS 2 Communication Vocabulary
slides.thesis: ROS 2 provides four primary communication paradigms. Choosing the right one is essential for robot performance.

rows: High-frequency sensor data, joint states, camera streams.
Quick configuration changes, triggering state transitions.
Long-running tasks like navigation goals or complex gaits.
Setting PID gains, safety thresholds, or network ports.

slides.bottom_band: Design Rule: Never use Services for high-frequency data streams. Use Topics instead.

slides.title: ROS 2 Quality of Service (QoS)
slides.thesis: QoS profiles decide how messages are buffered, retried, and delivered across the network.

board_data.label: Reliable vs Best Effort
board_data.value: Reliable guarantees delivery (best for commands). Best Effort drops lost packets to maintain speed (best for high-frequency sensors).

board_data.label: History & Depth
board_data.value: Keep Last (with depth N) buffers the last N messages. Keep All buffers everything until memory is full.

board_data.label: Transient Local Durability
board_data.value: Allows late-joining subscribers to receive the last published message (essential for static maps or robot URDF).

slides.bottom_band: Debugging Habit: Incompatible QoS profiles between publishers and subscribers will cause topics to show 0 active messages.

slides.title: Understanding ROS 2 Message Arrays
slides.thesis: Robotic state data is inherently repetitive. We use typed, structured arrays to handle bulk sensor and motor values.
board_data: Repetitive Structure: A quadruped has 12 joints, 4 feet, and multiple IMU axes. Storing these as individual variables is inefficient.
Typed Arrays: Standard ROS 2 messages (like sensor_msgs/JointState) use float64[] arrays for position, velocity, and effort.
Index Mapping: You must strictly follow the index mapping (e.g., index 0 = FL_hip, index 1 = FL_thigh) to avoid commanding the wrong motor.
Array Safety: Always verify that the array size matches your expected size before accessing elements to prevent runtime segmentation faults.
slides.bottom_band: Array Rule: Never hardcode array indices in multiple places. Define clear constant mappings or enums.

slides.title: RViz 2 Visualization Mental Model
slides.thesis: RViz is not a simulator; it is a 3D visualization window that displays what the robot believes is happening based on active TF frames.

board_data.label: Robot Model Display
board_data.value: Loads the URDF file to render the robot's physical links and joint positions in 3D space.

board_data.label: Coordinate Frames (TF)
board_data.value: Displays the transform tree (base_link, odom, map, hip, foot) and how they move relative to each other.

board_data.label: Sensor Visualizations
board_data.value: Overlays laser scans (LaserScan), point clouds (PointCloud2), and camera feeds directly into the 3D scene.

slides.bottom_band: Mental Model: If RViz shows the robot drifting, check the Odometry (odom) publisher, not the physical motors.

slides.title: Coordinate Transforms (TF) & Frames
slides.thesis: The TF tree is the spatial backbone of the robot. Every sensor reading must be transformed into a common frame to be useful.

steps: 1. Read sensor point P_robot relative to the camera or LiDAR frame.
2. Lookup transform T from sensor frame to base_link (defined in URDF).
3. Lookup transform T from base_link to odom (published by state estimator).
4. Lookup transform T from odom to map (published by SLAM node).

slides.bottom_band: TF Rule: A healthy TF tree must have a single, unbroken path from any child frame back to the root frame (map).

slides.title: RViz Diagnostic Habits
slides.thesis: When RViz displays errors or warnings, follow a structured diagnostic flow instead of randomly changing configurations.

rows: Fixed Frame does not exist in the active TF tree.
Change 'Fixed Frame' in RViz global options to 'odom' or 'base_link'.
The robot_description parameter was not published.
Verify that the 'robot_state_publisher' node is running and active.
No transform from lidar_frame to Fixed Frame.
Check if static_transform_publisher is running or if URDF is missing the link.
QoS mismatch or incorrect topic name subscribed.
Run 'ros2 topic info' to verify publisher and subscriber QoS settings.

slides.bottom_band: Diagnostic Rule: Always check the terminal logs of your launch file alongside the RViz UI warnings.

labs.title: Lab 0 — Environment, Architecture & Readiness
labs.content:
# Lab 0 — Environment, Architecture & Readiness

**Duration:** ~45–60 min (theory + checks)  
**Robot required:** Optional for Sections A–C; **required** for Section D  
**Motion:** None

**PDF coverage (Day 1):** GO2 introduction · development environment · basic operations

This lab is **Python + CycloneDDS only**. Optional ROS 2 (RViz, Nav2, etc.): [`docs/ROS2-INSTALL.md`](../../../docs/ROS2-INSTALL.md) at the repo root.

---

## Learning objectives

By the end of Lab 0 you should be able to:

1. Describe how your PC talks to Go2 over **Ethernet** and **DDS** (not ROS).
2. Explain why Go2 code uses **`unitree_go`** IDL types and **`SportClient`** for high-level control.
3. Install and verify **`unitree_sdk2_python`** + CycloneDDS on your machine.
4. Run readiness checks and interpret **PASS** / **PARTIAL** / **FAIL**.
5. State safety rules before any lab that sends motion commands (Lab 2+).

---

## 1. Go2 system architecture (conceptual)

The Go2 is a quadruped with an onboard computer (typically Jetson) on a fixed subnet. Your laptop joins that LAN over **wired Ethernet**.

```
┌─────────────────┐     Ethernet      ┌──────────────────────────────┐
│  Your PC        │   192.168.123.x   │  Go2 onboard (Jetson)        │
│  Python SDK     │ ◄──────────────► │  192.168.123.161 (typical)   │
│  CycloneDDS     │   UDP multicast   │  DDS topics + RPC services   │
└─────────────────┘                   └──────────────────────────────┘
```

| Role | Typical address | Notes |
|------|-----------------|-------|
| Your PC | e.g. `192.168.123.98/24` | Static IP on the port cabled to the robot |
| Go2 onboard | `192.168.123.161` | Ping before trusting DDS |
| Interface arg | e.g. `en6` (macOS), `enp0s31f6` (Linux) | Passed to `ChannelFactoryInitialize(0, iface)` — **not** the robot IP |

**Do not** pass `192.168.123.161` into `ChannelFactoryInitialize`. DDS discovery uses multicast on the robot subnet once your PC is on the correct NIC.

### Communication model (SDK2)

| Pattern | Use on Go2 | Example |
|---------|------------|---------|
| **Topic subscribe** | High-rate robot state | `rt/sportmodestate` → `SportModeState_` |
| **Topic subscribe** | Joints, IMU, power | `rt/lowstate` → `LowState_` (`unitree_go`) |
| **RPC client** | High-level sport commands | `SportClient` → `Move`, `StandUp`, … |

The Python SDK mirrors the C++ SDK2: same topic names and RPC clients as upstream examples under `example/go2/`.

### Go2 vs G1 in this camp (do not mix)

| | Go2 (Day 1) | G1 (Day 5+) |
|--|-------------|-------------|
| IDL | **`unitree_go`** | **`unitree_hg`** |
| High-level client | **`SportClient`** | **`LocoClient`** |
| Primary state topic (labs) | **`rt/sportmodestate`** | **`rt/lowstate`** |
| `CheckMode` name | Often **`mcf`** | Expect **`ai`** |

If G1 and Go2 share a lab network, read [G1 vs Go2 §4](../../../docs/G1-vs-GO2.md#4-lab-topology-one-switch-vs-two) (duplicate `.161` pitfall).

---

## 2. Software stack

```
Your lab scripts (course/day-01/)
        │
        ▼
unitree_sdk2py          ← pip install -e vendor/unitree_sdk2_python
        │
        ├── core.channel       ChannelFactoryInitialize, pub/sub
        ├── go2.sport            SportClient (high-level locomotion)
        ├── go2.obstacles_avoid  (Lab 4)
        ├── go2.video, vui, robot_state
        └── idl.unitree_go       SportModeState_, LowState_, …
        │
        ▼
CycloneDDS 0.10.x       ← CYCLONEDDS_HOME must point to install prefix
```

### 2.1 Clone and install (once per machine)

From the **repo root**:

```bash
conda create -n unitree_env python=3.11 -y
conda activate unitree_env
cd /path/to/vinci-unitree

cd vendor && git clone https://github.com/unitreerobotics/unitree_sdk2_python.git && cd ..
./scripts/setup_unitree_sdk.sh
```

Add to `~/.bashrc` or `~/.zshrc`:

```bash
export CYCLONEDDS_HOME="$HOME/cyclonedds/install"
```

Official install FAQ (CycloneDDS not found):  
https://github.com/unitreerobotics/unitree_sdk2_python#faq

### 2.2 Lab 0 script — verify **without** the robot

```bash
conda activate unitree_env
export CYCLONEDDS_HOME="$HOME/cyclonedds/install"
unset CYCLONEDDS_URI
cd /path/to/vinci-unitree

python course/day-01/lab-00/lab00_readiness.py
```

**Expected:** environment PASS, vendor tree PASS, Go2 imports PASS, `verify_install.py` PASS, summary **machine ready**.

Equivalent manual check:

```bash
python scripts/verify_install.py
python scripts/verify_install.py --dds-test   # optional local pub/sub
```

### 2.3 Explore the upstream Go2 layout

After clone, skim these paths (read-only — do not edit `vendor/`):

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `example/go2/high_level/go2_sport_client.py` | Interactive sport menu (Lab 2) |
| `example/go2/front_camera/camera_opencv.py` | Front camera (Lab 3) |
| `example/obstacles_avoid/obstacles_avoid_switch.py` | Avoidance (Lab 4) |
| `unitree_sdk2py/go2/sport/sport_client.py` | `SportClient` API |

Optional DDS tutorial (no robot): `example/helloworld/publisher.py` + `subscriber.py` in two terminals.

---

## 3. High-level sport API (preview)

Day 1 Labs 2–4 use **`SportClient`** — RPC to the onboard sport service.

| Category | Methods (examples) | Notes |
|----------|-------------------|--------|
| **Posture** | `StandUp()`, `StandDown()`, `BalanceStand()`, `RecoveryStand()` | Lab 2 menu |
| **Velocity** | `Move(vx, vy, vyaw)`, `StopMove()` | Sustained move may need periodic sends (upstream docs) |
| **Modes** | `Damp()`, `FreeAvoid(True/False)` | **Avoid `Damp()`** in labs unless recovering |
| **Readiness** | `Init()`, timeout via `SetTimeout()` | Used in connection check |

Menu ids in `go2_sport_client.py` (for reference):

| Id | Name | Lab use |
|----|------|---------|
| 9 | `balanced stand` | Safe first motion (Lab 2) |
| 1 | `stand_up` | OK with supervision |
| 6 | `stop_move` | Stop after move tests |
| 0 | `damp` | **Avoid** — blocks normal sport until recovered |
| 11–12, 19 | flips / jump | **Not for training** |

Details: [Sports services](https://support.unitree.com/home/en/developer/sports_services) · [`docs/GO2-FIELD-GUIDE.md`](../../../docs/GO2-FIELD-GUIDE.md)

---

## 4. Safety and operating rules

Read before any lab that moves the robot.

1. **Clear space** — ~2 m around the dog; flat floor; no loose cables.
2. **Wired Ethernet** — robot LAN only; confirm NIC with `ip -br addr` or `ifconfig`.
3. **Power-on wait** — ~1 minute after boot before RPC.
4. **Do not call `Damp()` casually** — recovery needs `stand_up`, `balanced stand`, or remote.
5. **High-level vs low-level** — Day 1 uses **sport** first. Low-level examples need sport disabled in the Unitree app (upstream `example/go2/low_level/`).
6. **Shared lab** — only one robot at `192.168.123.161` per switch, or renumber IPs — see [G1 vs Go2](../../../docs/G1-vs-GO2.md).
7. **Emergency** — know power switch and remote estop; spotter for first motion (Lab 2+).

Official sport example warning:

> *Please ensure there are no obstacles around the robot while running this example.*

---

## 5. Hands-on checklist

Work through **A → D**. Tick each box in your notes.

### A. Machine setup (no robot)

- [ ] `conda activate unitree_env`
- [ ] `export CYCLONEDDS_HOME="$HOME/cyclonedds/install"`
- [ ] `unset CYCLONEDDS_URI`
- [ ] `python course/day-01/lab-00/lab00_readiness.py` → **machine ready**
- [ ] `vendor/unitree_sdk2_python/example/go2/high_level/go2_sport_client.py` exists

### B. Network preparation (robot powered, PC wired)

Replace `en6` with your interface (`enp0s31f6` on Linux — see [Go2 Field Guide §macOS](../../../docs/GO2-FIELD-GUIDE.md)).

- [ ] Static IP on robot subnet, e.g. `192.168.123.98/24`
- [ ] `ping -c 2 192.168.123.161` → replies
- [ ] Cable goes to **this** Go2 (not another robot on the same `.161`)

### C. Session environment (every robot day)

```bash
conda activate unitree_env
export CYCLONEDDS_HOME="$HOME/cyclonedds/install"
unset CYCLONEDDS_URI
cd /path/to/vinci-unitree
```

- [ ] Both exports active in the shell you will use for labs

### D. Readiness check (robot on)

```bash
python course/day-01/lab-00/lab00_readiness.py en6
```

Or:

```bash
./scripts/go2_quickstart.sh en6
# python scripts/go2_connection_check.py --interface en6
```

| Result | Meaning | Next step |
|--------|---------|-----------|
| **PASS** (exit 0) | SDK, IP, ping, `sportmodestate`, `lowstate`, `SportClient.Init` | [Lab 1](../lab-01/) |
| **PARTIAL** (exit 2) | DDS + sport OK; unusual `CheckMode` | Read log; may still proceed to Lab 1; fix before Lab 2 motion |
| **FAIL** (exit 1) | SDK, IP, ping, or no DDS | [GO2 Field Guide](../../../docs/GO2-FIELD-GUIDE.md); do not run motion labs |

**Note:** Go2 `CheckMode` name **`mcf`** is normal — do not apply G1’s `'ai'` rule.

---

## 6. Knowledge check (self-test)

Answer without looking, then verify against Sections 1–3 or the field guide.

1. Which IDL package does Go2 use — `unitree_go` or `unitree_hg`?
2. Which DDS topic is the primary **high-level** sport state stream on Go2?
3. Which RPC client drives stand / walk / tricks in Day 1?
4. What onboard IP do you ping before trusting DDS?
5. Why must `CYCLONEDDS_URI` usually be **unset** when passing the interface name to `ChannelFactoryInitialize(0, iface)`?

<details>
<summary>Answers</summary>

1. **`unitree_go`**
2. **`rt/sportmodestate`** (`SportModeState_`)
3. **`SportClient`**
4. **`192.168.123.161`** (typical)
5. A preset URI can bind DDS to the wrong interface; camp examples use the **NIC name** only

</details>

---

## 7. Deliverable

For Lab 0 submission (notebook / wiki / PR comment):

- Log snippet: `lab00_readiness.py` (no iface) → **machine ready**
- Log snippet: `lab00_readiness.py en6` → **PASS** or **PARTIAL** with one-line explanation
- Your PC **interface name** and **IP** on `192.168.123.0/24`
- One sentence: what you would check if `rt/sportmodestate` never arrives

---

## 8. Next lab

**[Lab 1 — DDS observation (`rt/sportmodestate`)](../lab-01/)**  
Subscribe to sport mode state, estimate rate, and optional `rt/lowstate` — still no motion commands.

---

## References

- Repo: [GO2 Field Guide](../../../docs/GO2-FIELD-GUIDE.md) · [G1 vs Go2](../../../docs/G1-vs-GO2.md)
- Script: [`lab00_readiness.py`](lab00_readiness.py) · [`scripts/go2_connection_check.py`](../../../scripts/go2_connection_check.py)
- Upstream: [unitree_sdk2_python](https://github.com/unitreerobotics/unitree_sdk2_python) · [Quick start](https://support.unitree.com/home/en/developer/Quick_start)

code_files.name: lab00_readiness.py
code_files.code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Lab 0 — Go2 environment, SDK layout, and optional on-robot readiness.

No motion commands. Use this script for Lab 0 deliverables.

Usage (from repo root):
  conda activate unitree_env
  export CYCLONEDDS_HOME="$HOME/cyclonedds/install"

  # A. Machine only (no robot)
  python course/day-01/lab-00/lab00_readiness.py

  # B. Robot on, wired to Go2
  python course/day-01/lab-00/lab00_readiness.py en6
  python course/day-01/lab-00/lab00_readiness.py en6 --skip-multicast

Also runs scripts/verify_install.py import checks unless --skip-verify.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import os
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]
SCRIPTS_DIR = REPO_ROOT / "scripts"
VENDOR_SDK = REPO_ROOT / "vendor" / "unitree_sdk2_python"

GO2_EXAMPLE_PATHS = (
    "example/go2/high_level/go2_sport_client.py",
    "example/go2/front_camera/camera_opencv.py",
    "example/obstacles_avoid/obstacles_avoid_switch.py",
    "unitree_sdk2py/go2/sport/sport_client.py",
)


def _ok(msg: str) -> None:
    print(f"  PASS  {msg}")


def _fail(msg: str) -> None:
    print(f"  FAIL  {msg}")


def check_cyclonedds_home() -> bool:
    home = os.environ.get("CYCLONEDDS_HOME")
    if not home:
        _fail("CYCLONEDDS_HOME is not set")
        print("        export CYCLONEDDS_HOME=\"$HOME/cyclonedds/install\"")
        return False
    if not Path(home).is_dir():
        _fail(f"CYCLONEDDS_HOME path missing: {home}")
        return False
    _ok(f"CYCLONEDDS_HOME={home}")
    return True


def check_cyclonedds_uri() -> bool:
    if os.environ.get("CYCLONEDDS_URI"):
        print("  WARN  CYCLONEDDS_URI is set — unset before robot labs:")
        print("        unset CYCLONEDDS_URI")
        return True
    _ok("CYCLONEDDS_URI unset")
    return True


def check_vendor_tree() -> bool:
    ok = True
    if not VENDOR_SDK.is_dir():
        _fail(f"SDK not cloned: {VENDOR_SDK}")
        print("        cd vendor && git clone https://github.com/unitreerobotics/unitree_sdk2_python.git")
        return False
    _ok(f"vendor SDK: {VENDOR_SDK}")
    for rel in GO2_EXAMPLE_PATHS:
        path = VENDOR_SDK / rel
        if path.is_file():
            _ok(rel)
        else:
            _fail(f"missing: {rel}")
            ok = False
    return ok


def check_go2_imports() -> bool:
    ok = True
    try:
        import cyclonedds  # noqa: F401

        _ok(f"cyclonedds {getattr(cyclonedds, '__version__', '?')}")
    except ImportError as e:
        _fail(f"cyclonedds: {e}")
        ok = False

    try:
        import unitree_sdk2py

        _ok(f"unitree_sdk2py ({unitree_sdk2py.__file__})")
    except ImportError as e:
        _fail(f"unitree_sdk2py: {e}")
        return False

    try:
        from unitree_sdk2py.idl.unitree_go.msg.dds_ import LowState_, SportModeState_

        _ = (LowState_, SportModeState_)
        _ok("idl.unitree_go (SportModeState_, LowState_)")
    except ImportError as e:
        _fail(f"unitree_go IDL: {e}")
        ok = False

    try:
        from unitree_sdk2py.idl.unitree_hg.msg.dds_ import LowState_ as HgLowState_

        _ = HgLowState_
        _ok("idl.unitree_hg importable (G1 only — do not use on Go2)")
    except ImportError:
        print("  WARN  unitree_hg not found (OK if SDK variant omits HG)")

    try:
        from unitree_sdk2py.go2.sport.sport_client import SportClient

        _ = SportClient
        _ok("go2.sport.SportClient")
    except ImportError as e:
        _fail(f"SportClient: {e}")
        ok = False

    return ok


def run_verify_install() -> bool:
    script = SCRIPTS_DIR / "verify_install.py"
    if not script.is_file():
        _fail(f"verify_install.py not found: {script}")
        return False
    print("\n=== scripts/verify_install.py ===")
    r = subprocess.run(
        [sys.executable, str(script)],
        cwd=REPO_ROOT,
        check=False,
    )
    return r.returncode == 0


def run_go2_connection_check(iface: str, skip_multicast: bool) -> int:
    script = SCRIPTS_DIR / "go2_connection_check.py"
    if not script.is_file():
        _fail(f"go2_connection_check.py not found: {script}")
        return 1
    cmd = [sys.executable, str(script), "--interface", iface]
    if skip_multicast:
        cmd.append("--skip-multicast")
    print(f"\n=== scripts/go2_connection_check.py ({iface}) ===")
    return subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=REPO_ROOT, check=False).returncode


def print_stack_reminder() -> None:
    print(
        """
=== Go2 Python stack (Lab 0) ===

  course/day-01/lab-*.py
           │
           ▼
  unitree_sdk2py
    ├── core.channel      ChannelFactoryInitialize, pub/sub
    ├── go2.sport         SportClient (high-level locomotion)
    ├── go2.obstacles_avoid, video, vui, robot_state
    └── idl.unitree_go    SportModeState_, LowState_, …
           │
           ▼
  CycloneDDS 0.10.x       CYCLONEDDS_HOME

Topics (subscribe):  rt/sportmodestate, rt/lowstate
RPC (Lab 2+):        SportClient → sport service

Onboard (typical):   192.168.123.161  |  PC: 192.168.123.x/24
"""
    )


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    parser.add_argument(
        "interface",
        nargs="?",
        default=None,
        help="Ethernet NIC to Go2 (e.g. en6). Omit for machine-only checks.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--skip-verify",
        action="store_true",
        help="Skip scripts/verify_install.py",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--skip-multicast",
        action="store_true",
        help="Pass --skip-multicast to go2_connection_check.py",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    print("Lab 0 — Go2 readiness")
    print(f"  repo: {REPO_ROOT}")

    failed = False

    print("\n=== Environment ===")
    failed |= not check_cyclonedds_home()
    check_cyclonedds_uri()

    print("\n=== Vendor tree ===")
    failed |= not check_vendor_tree()

    print("\n=== Go2 Python imports ===")
    failed |= not check_go2_imports()

    if not args.skip_verify:
        if not run_verify_install():
            failed = True

    print_stack_reminder()

    if failed:
        print("Summary: FAIL — fix install (./scripts/setup_unitree_sdk.sh)")
        return 1

    if args.interface is None:
        print("Summary: PASS — machine ready (no robot checks)")
        print("  Next: wire Go2, then re-run:")
        print("        python course/day-01/lab-00/lab00_readiness.py en6")
        return 0

    code = run_go2_connection_check(args.interface, args.skip_multicast)
    if code == 0:
        print("\nLab 0 complete — proceed to Lab 1")
    elif code == 2:
        print("\nLab 0 PARTIAL — DDS OK; review CheckMode in log, then Lab 1")
    else:
        print("\nLab 0 FAIL — see docs/GO2-FIELD-GUIDE.md")
    return code


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

labs.title: Lab 1 — ROS 2 architecture & workspace
labs.content:
# Lab 1 — ROS 2 architecture & workspace

**TtT Day 1 · 10:45–12:30**  
**Robot required:** No  
**Motion:** None

## Objectives

- Explain nodes, topics, services, and parameters in ROS 2.
- Install/source ROS 2 Humble and relate it to Unitree DDS on the robot LAN.
- Run a minimal workspace check (no robot required for first pass).

## Steps

1. Follow [`docs/ROS2-INSTALL.md`](../../../docs/ROS2-INSTALL.md) on Ubuntu 22.04 (instructor machine or lab PC).
2. Verify: `source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash` → `ros2 run demo_nodes_cpp talker` (Ctrl+C).
3. Read the SDK ↔ ROS bridge diagram in [`day-01/README.md`](../README.md).
4. *(When `unitree_ros2` is built)* `source ../../../scripts/setup_unitree_ros2.sh <iface>` → `ros2 topic list` with robot powered.

## Deliverable

One-paragraph note: how a ROS 2 topic name relates to a DDS topic such as `rt/sportmodestate` on Go2.

**Next:** [Lab 2 — DDS subscribe (Go2 communication)](../lab-02/)

labs.title: Lab 1 — DDS Observation (`rt/sportmodestate`)
labs.content:
# Lab 1 — DDS Observation (`rt/sportmodestate`)

**Duration:** ~30–45 min  
**Robot motion:** None — subscribe only  
**Prerequisites:** [Lab 0](../lab-00/) connection check **PASS** on your interface

**PDF coverage:** Topics & messages · managing sensor data (state streams)

---

## Learning objectives

1. Explain the difference between **DDS topics** (streaming state) and **RPC** (request/response commands).
2. Subscribe to `rt/sportmodestate` with the correct Go2 type: `SportModeState_` (`unitree_go`).
3. Optionally subscribe to `rt/lowstate` with **`unitree_go`** `LowState_` (not G1’s `unitree_hg`).
4. Read `mode`, `gait_type`, velocity, and position from live sport messages.
5. Estimate publish rate and optionally save a **JSONL log** for inspection workflows.

---

## 1. Concepts

### Pub/sub vs RPC

| Pattern | Direction | Go2 example | Used for |
|---------|-----------|-------------|----------|
| **Topic subscribe** | Robot → PC (stream) | `rt/sportmodestate` | High-level sport / gait state |
| **Topic subscribe** | Robot → PC | `rt/lowstate` | Joints, IMU, BMS (proprioception) |
| **RPC client** | PC ↔ Robot | `SportClient` | Stand, move, tricks (Lab 2+) |

Lab 1 uses **only** pub/sub. You are listening; you are not commanding motion.

### Topics and types (Go2)

| Topic | Python type | IDL package |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| `rt/sportmodestate` | `SportModeState_` | **`unitree_go`** |
| `rt/lowstate` | `LowState_` | **`unitree_go`** |

G1 camp uses the same topic name `rt/lowstate` with a **different** message layout (`unitree_hg`). Wrong type → no messages or garbage.

### `SportModeState_` fields (useful for Day 1)

| Field | Role |
|-------|------|
| `mode` | Sport / behaviour mode id |
| `gait_type` | Gait classification |
| `progress` | Motion task progress |
| `error_code` | Sport error flag |
| `body_height` | Body height estimate |
| `velocity` | `[vx, vy, vz]`-style body velocity components |
| `position` | Position components (frame per firmware) |
| `yaw_speed` | Yaw rate |
| `imu_state` | IMU snapshot inside sport state |

`rt/lowstate` adds `tick`, per-motor `motor_state`, `bms_state`, etc. — use `--lowstate` to print one sample.

### Managing sensor data

Treat DDS streams as **observations** you can log for inspection:

| Stream | Inspection role |
|--------|-----------------|
| `sportmodestate` | Where the dog thinks it is in sport mode, gait, velocity |
| `lowstate` | Joint/IMU health, power, `tick` for freshness |

Camp script supports `--log sportmodestate_log.jsonl` (one JSON object per printed line).

### DDS session (same as Lab 0)

```bash
conda activate unitree_env
export CYCLONEDDS_HOME="$HOME/cyclonedds/install"
unset CYCLONEDDS_URI
```

```python
ChannelFactoryInitialize(0, "en6")  # your interface
sub = ChannelSubscriber("rt/sportmodestate", SportModeState_)
sub.Init(callback, 10)  # queue depth 10
```

Official hello-world (no robot): `vendor/unitree_sdk2_python/example/helloworld/`

---

## 2. Hands-on

### Step 1 — Quick readiness

```bash
cd /path/to/vinci-unitree
python course/day-01/lab-00/lab00_readiness.py en6
# or:
python scripts/go2_connection_check.py --interface en6 --skip-multicast
```

Expect **PASS** (or fix network/DDS per Lab 0).

### Step 2 — First message

```bash
python course/day-01/lab-02/lab01_listen_sportmodestate.py en6 --once
```

**Expected:** `First rt/sportmodestate received:` with `mode`, `gait_type`, `vel`, `pos`, etc.

### Step 3 — Stream for 10 seconds

```bash
python course/day-01/lab-02/lab01_listen_sportmodestate.py en6 --duration 15 --rate 1
```

Note the approximate **Hz** printed at the end. Print rate is throttled by `--rate`; underlying DDS rate is usually higher.

### Step 4 — Lowstate + log (inspection data bundle)

```bash
python course/day-01/lab-02/lab01_listen_sportmodestate.py en6 --duration 20 --rate 1 \
  --lowstate --log sportmodestate_log.jsonl
```

Confirm `sportmodestate_log.jsonl` exists and contains JSON lines with `mode`, `velocity`, `position`.

### Step 4b — Inspection fields on `lowstate` *(optional)*

```bash
python course/day-01/lab-02/lab01_listen_sportmodestate.py en6 --once --inspect
```

**Expected:** first `lowstate` line includes `power_v`, `bms_soc`, and `wireless_remote` bytes (remote vs API debugging).

### Step 5 — Compare with connection check

`go2_connection_check.py` already subscribes to both topics for PASS. Lab 1 exposes the **same data path** with readable fields — the split before `SportClient` motion in Lab 2 is intentional.

---

## 3. Exercises

### Exercise A — Rate

Run with `--duration 30` and record message count and Hz. What happens if you unplug Ethernet mid-run?

### Exercise B — Field watch

While the robot is standing still, note `mode`, `gait_type`, and `velocity`. When a teammate gently commands a small move (Lab 2, supervised), do `velocity` components change on the next Lab 1 run?

### Exercise C — Pub/sub vs RPC (written)

In 2–3 sentences: why does `go2_connection_check.py` use **both** `ChannelSubscriber("rt/sportmodestate")` and `SportClient.Init()`?

<details>
<summary>Hint</summary>

`sportmodestate` proves the DDS **streaming** path is alive; `SportClient` proves the sport **RPC** service answers — different channel than the topic alone.

</details>

### Exercise D — Wrong IDL (discussion)

What symptom would you expect if you used `unitree_hg.LowState_` on Go2 `rt/lowstate`?

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

No valid messages, decode errors, or nonsense values — always match IDL package to robot line ([G1 vs Go2](../../../docs/G1-vs-GO2.md)).

</details>

---

## 4. Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Action |
|---------|--------|
| No message in 8s | Re-run [Lab 0](../lab-00/); confirm iface and robot power |
| `ModuleNotFoundError: unitree_sdk2py` | `conda activate unitree_env`; `./scripts/setup_unitree_sdk.sh` |
| Wrong interface on macOS | `ifconfig \| grep 192.168.123` → often `en6` |
| `--lowstate` never prints | Wait full `--duration`; lowstate may arrive after sport; re-run with `--once --lowstate` |

---

## 5. Deliverable

Submit:

- Log snippet: `lab01_listen_sportmodestate.py en6 --once` (first line)
- Approximate Hz from a 15s run
- Optional: first line of `sportmodestate_log.jsonl`
- Answer to Exercise C

---

## 6. Next lab

**[Lab 2 — Sport RPC & readiness](../lab-02/)**  
`SportClient`, `CheckMode`, and supervised sport menu — still minimal motion.

---

## References

- [Lab 0](../lab-00/) · [`lab01_listen_sportmodestate.py`](lab01_listen_sportmodestate.py)
- [GO2 Field Guide](../../../docs/GO2-FIELD-GUIDE.md) · [G1 vs Go2](../../../docs/G1-vs-GO2.md)
- [G1 Lab 1 (pattern)](../../day-05/lab-01/)

code_files.name: lab01_listen_sportmodestate.py
code_files.code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Lab 1 — Subscribe to Go2 rt/sportmodestate (and optional rt/lowstate). No motion commands.

Usage:
  conda activate unitree_env
  export CYCLONEDDS_HOME="$HOME/cyclonedds/install"
  unset CYCLONEDDS_URI
  python course/day-01/lab-02/lab01_listen_sportmodestate.py en6
  python course/day-01/lab-02/lab01_listen_sportmodestate.py en6 --duration 15 --rate 1
  python course/day-01/lab-02/lab01_listen_sportmodestate.py en6 --once
  python course/day-01/lab-02/lab01_listen_sportmodestate.py en6 --lowstate --log sportmodestate_log.jsonl
  python course/day-01/lab-02/lab01_listen_sportmodestate.py en6 --once --inspect
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import os
import sys
import time
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

from unitree_sdk2py.core.channel import ChannelFactoryInitialize, ChannelSubscriber
from unitree_sdk2py.idl.unitree_go.msg.dds_ import LowState_, SportModeState_


def _init_dds(iface: str) -> None:
    if os.environ.get("CYCLONEDDS_URI"):
        print("Warning: CYCLONEDDS_URI is set; unset it for interface-only init.")
        ChannelFactoryInitialize(0)
    else:
        ChannelFactoryInitialize(0, iface)


def _vec3(label: str, v: Any) -> str:
    try:
        return f"{label}=({float(v[0]):.3f},{float(v[1]):.3f},{float(v[2]):.3f})"
    except (TypeError, IndexError, ValueError):
        return f"{label}=?"


def _format_sport(msg: SportModeState_, count: int) -> str:
    vel = msg.velocity
    pos = msg.position
    return (
        f"[{count:4d}] mode={msg.mode} gait_type={msg.gait_type} "
        f"progress={msg.progress} error_code={msg.error_code} "
        f"body_height={msg.body_height:.3f} "
        f"{_vec3('vel', vel)} {_vec3('pos', pos)} "
        f"yaw_speed={msg.yaw_speed:.3f}"
    )


def _format_lowstate(msg: LowState_, count: int, *, inspect: bool = False) -> str:
    imu = msg.imu_state
    n_motors = len(msg.motor_state) if msg.motor_state else 0
    base = (
        f"[{count:4d}] tick={msg.tick} "
        f"{_vec3('imu_rpy', imu.rpy)} "
        f"motors={n_motors} bandwidth={msg.bandwidth}"
    )
    if not inspect:
        return base
    bms = msg.bms_state
    soc = getattr(bms, "soc", None)
    extra = (
        f" power_v={msg.power_v:.2f} power_a={msg.power_a:.2f}"
        f" bms_soc={soc}"
    )
    wr = msg.wireless_remote
    if wr is not None and len(wr) >= 4:
        extra += f" wireless_remote[0:4]={list(wr[:4])}"
    return base + extra


def _sport_record(msg: SportModeState_, count: int, t: float) -> dict[str, Any]:
    return {
        "t": round(t, 3),
        "n": count,
        "topic": "rt/sportmodestate",
        "mode": int(msg.mode),
        "gait_type": int(msg.gait_type),
        "progress": float(msg.progress),
        "error_code": int(msg.error_code),
        "body_height": float(msg.body_height),
        "yaw_speed": float(msg.yaw_speed),
        "velocity": [float(msg.velocity[i]) for i in range(3)],
        "position": [float(msg.position[i]) for i in range(3)],
    }


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    parser.add_argument(
        "interface",
        nargs="?",
        default=os.environ.get("GO2_INTERFACE", "enp0s31f6"),
        help="Ethernet interface to robot (e.g. en6 on macOS)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--duration",
        type=float,
        default=10.0,
        help="Seconds to listen after first sport message (default: 10)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--once",
        action="store_true",
        help="Print first sportmodestate message and exit",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--rate",
        type=float,
        default=2.0,
        help="Max print rate in Hz for sportmodestate (default: 2)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--lowstate",
        action="store_true",
        help="Also subscribe to rt/lowstate and print first sample",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--log",
        metavar="PATH",
        default=None,
        help="Append JSON lines (sportmodestate) at print rate",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--inspect",
        action="store_true",
        help="With --lowstate: print BMS/power/wireless_remote on first sample",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()
    if args.inspect:
        args.lowstate = True

    if not os.environ.get("CYCLONEDDS_HOME"):
        print("ERROR: CYCLONEDDS_HOME is not set. See Lab 0 / scripts/setup_unitree_sdk.sh")
        return 1

    print(f"Interface: {args.interface}")
    _init_dds(args.interface)

    sport_count = 0
    low_count = 0
    sport_done = False
    first_at: float | None = None
    last_print = 0.0
    min_interval = 1.0 / args.rate if args.rate > 0 else 0.0
    log_path: Path | None = Path(args.log) if args.log else None
    log_fp = None

    if log_path:
        log_fp = log_path.open("a", encoding="utf-8")
        print(f"Logging sportmodestate to {log_path}")

    topics = '"rt/sportmodestate" (SportModeState_, unitree_go)'
    if args.lowstate:
        topics += ' and "rt/lowstate" (LowState_, unitree_go)'
    print(f"Subscribing to {topics}...")

    def on_sport(msg: SportModeState_) -> None:
        nonlocal sport_count, first_at, last_print, sport_done
        if args.once and sport_done:
            return
        sport_count += 1
        now = time.time()
        if first_at is None:
            first_at = now
            last_print = now
            print("First rt/sportmodestate received:")
            print("  " + _format_sport(msg, sport_count))
            if log_fp:
                log_fp.write(json.dumps(_sport_record(msg, sport_count, now)) + "\n")
            if args.once:
                sport_done = True
                return
        if args.once:
            return
        if now - last_print >= min_interval:
            last_print = now
            print("  " + _format_sport(msg, sport_count))
            if log_fp:
                log_fp.write(json.dumps(_sport_record(msg, sport_count, now)) + "\n")

    def on_lowstate(msg: LowState_) -> None:
        nonlocal low_count
        low_count += 1
        if low_count == 1:
            print("First rt/lowstate received:")
            print(
                "  "
                + _format_lowstate(msg, low_count, inspect=args.inspect)
            )

    sport_sub = ChannelSubscriber("rt/sportmodestate", SportModeState_)
    sport_sub.Init(on_sport, 10)

    if args.lowstate:
        low_sub = ChannelSubscriber("rt/lowstate", LowState_)
        low_sub.Init(on_lowstate, 10)

    deadline = time.time() + 8.0
    while time.time() < deadline and sport_count == 0:
        time.sleep(0.05)

    if sport_count == 0:
        print(
            "FAIL: no sportmodestate in 8s. Run Lab 0:\n"
            f"  python course/day-01/lab-00/lab00_readiness.py {args.interface}"
        )
        if log_fp:
            log_fp.close()
        return 1

    if args.once:
        if args.lowstate and low_count == 0:
            deadline = time.time() + 2.0
            while time.time() < deadline and low_count == 0:
                time.sleep(0.05)
        if log_fp:
            log_fp.close()
        low_note = f", lowstate={'OK' if low_count else 'none'}" if args.lowstate else ""
        print(f"Done. sportmodestate=1{low_note}")
        return 0

    end = time.time() + args.duration
    while time.time() < end:
        time.sleep(0.05)

    elapsed = time.time() - (first_at or time.time())
    hz = sport_count / elapsed if elapsed > 0 else 0.0
    print(
        f"Done. sportmodestate: {sport_count} messages in {elapsed:.1f}s (~{hz:.0f} Hz)"
    )
    if args.lowstate:
        print(f"      lowstate: {low_count} messages in {elapsed:.1f}s")
    if log_fp:
        log_fp.close()
        print(f"Log appended: {log_path}")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

labs.title: Lab 2 — Sport RPC Readiness & Supervised Menu
labs.content:
# Lab 2 — Sport RPC Readiness & Supervised Menu

**Duration:** ~45 min  
**Robot motion:** **Step 2** scripted posture (and optional short walk) · **Step 3** optional sport menu  
**Prerequisites:** [Lab 1](../lab-01/) complete (`sportmodestate` streaming)

**PDF coverage:** Services (RPC) · basic operations

---

## Learning objectives

1. Relate **`SportClient`** to request/response **RPC** (distinct from DDS topics in Lab 1).
2. Run **`lab02_sport_readonly.py`** — DDS + `CheckMode` + `SportClient.Init` without motion.
3. Interpret Go2 **`CheckMode`** (often **`mcf`**, not G1’s **`ai`**).
4. Run **`lab02_safe_posture.py`** — first **visible** motion (`StandUp` + walk by default).
5. Optionally use the official **`go2_sport_client.py`** menu for extra commands.
6. List **forbidden** menu ids before Lab 4 avoid move.

---

## 1. Concepts

### Two layers you already saw in Lab 1

| Layer | Mechanism | Lab |
|-------|-----------|-----|
| **Streaming state** | DDS topics `rt/sportmodestate`, `rt/lowstate` | Lab 1 |
| **Commands / queries** | RPC clients (`SportClient`, `MotionSwitcherClient`) | Lab 2 |

`go2_connection_check.py` uses **both**: topics prove the wire is alive; RPC proves the sport **service** answers.

### SportClient

| Item | Value |
|------|--------|
| Class | `unitree_sdk2py.go2.sport.sport_client.SportClient` |
| Service | `"sport"` (same name family as G1 loco, different API surface) |
| Setup | `SetTimeout(10.0)` → `Init()` |
| Step 1 | **Read-only**: `Init()`, `AutoRecoveryGet()` in `lab02_sport_readonly.py` |
| Step 2 | **`StandUp()` → `BalanceStand()` → walk** in `lab02_safe_posture.py` (default) |
| Step 3 (optional) | Interactive menu — `go2_sport_client.py` |

Return **code `0`** means the service accepted the call; always watch the physical robot.

### Motion switcher — `CheckMode()`

```python
msc = MotionSwitcherClient()
msc.SetTimeout(10.0)
msc.Init()
code, result = msc.CheckMode()   # result e.g. {'name': 'mcf', 'form': '0'}
```

| `name` (examples) | On Go2 |
|-------------------|--------|
| **`mcf`** | Common — **PASS** for this camp |
| `ai` | Also accepted |
| Other / empty | **WARN** in lab script; sport may still work |

Do **not** require G1’s `'ai'` only — see [G1 vs Go2](../../../docs/G1-vs-GO2.md).

### Official sport menu (`go2_sport_client.py`)

| Id | Name | Day 1 |
|----|------|-------|
| **1** | `stand_up` → `StandUp()` | Stand **from the ground** (not handstand) |
| **9** | `balanced stand` → `BalanceStand()` | Balanced standing |
| **6** | `stop_move` | Use after any move test |
| **7** | `hand stand` → `HandStand()` | **Acrobatic — not used in camp** |
| **3–5** | forward / lateral / rotate | **Instructor only**, short |
| **0** | `damp` | **Do not use** — hard to recover in class |
| **11–12, 19** | flips / free jump | **Forbidden** |

See also [Day 1 sport command table](../README.md#sport-commands-go2--quick-reference).

Docs: [sports_services](https://support.unitree.com/home/en/developer/sports_services)

---

## 2. Hands-on

### Step 1 — Read-only readiness (no motion)

```bash
conda activate unitree_env
export CYCLONEDDS_HOME="$HOME/cyclonedds/install"
unset CYCLONEDDS_URI
cd /path/to/vinci-unitree

python course/day-01/lab-03/lab02_sport_readonly.py en6
```

**Expected sections:**

```text
[DDS topics]
  rt/sportmodestate: OK (...)
  rt/lowstate:       OK (...)

[Motion switcher RPC]
  CheckMode: OK  name='mcf'  ...

[SportClient RPC — service "sport"]
  Init: OK
  AutoRecoveryGet: OK  enabled=...

Readiness: PASS — OK for supervised sport menu ...
```

| Exit | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **0** | PASS |
| **2** | PARTIAL — unusual `CheckMode`; review log |
| **1** | FAIL — fix Lab 0 |

Optional — watch RPC while someone else uses the remote (read-only):

```bash
python course/day-01/lab-03/lab02_sport_readonly.py en6 --watch 10
```

### Step 2 — First **visible** motion (required)

**This is the first lab script that moves the robot.** Labs 0–1 and Lab 3 are observe-only.

**Default sequence** (see [`go2_motion_helpers.py`](../go2_motion_helpers.py)):

1. **`StandUp()`** — wait **5 s** (dog leaves the ground — most visible step)  
2. **`BalanceStand()`** — wait **2 s**  
3. **`Move(0.3, 0, 0)`** @ 20 Hz for **3 s**  
4. **`StopMove()`**

```bash
python course/day-01/lab-03/lab02_safe_posture.py en6 --dry-run
python course/day-01/lab-03/lab02_safe_posture.py en6
```

Posture only (no forward walk):

```bash
python course/day-01/lab-03/lab02_safe_posture.py en6 --posture-only
```

| Flag | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `--dry-run` | Print plan only |
| `--posture-only` | StandUp + BalanceStand only (skip walk) |
| `--skip-stand` | Skip stand prep (robot already up) |
| `--vx` / `--move-sec` / `--move-hz` | Walk (defaults **0.3 m/s**, **3 s**) |
| `--stand-up-wait` | Seconds after StandUp (default **5**) |
| `-y` | Skip `yes` prompt (instructor) |

`code=0` means RPC accepted — **watch the robot**, especially during **StandUp**.

### Step 2b — Showcase (“wow”) gestures *(optional, after Step 2)*

Supervised sport tricks using more of **`SportClient`** — great for student engagement.

```bash
# Safe defaults: Hello → Stretch → Heart (robot must be standing)
python course/day-01/lab-03/lab02_showcase.py en6 --skip-stand

# Full sequence from ground
python course/day-01/lab-03/lab02_showcase.py en6

# Instructor only — handstand requires typing "handstand" after "yes"
python course/day-01/lab-03/lab02_showcase.py en6 --wow-handstand --skip-stand
```

| Flag | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `--gestures hello,stretch,heart` | Pick gestures |
| `--wow-handstand` | `HandStand(True)` hold → `HandStand(False)` → `BalanceStand()` |
| `--skip-stand` | Use if Step 2 already ran |
| `--dry-run` | Print plan only |

**Safety:** flat floor, ~3 m clear for handstand; spotter; never combine with damp or flips in class.

Optional gait before walk in Step 2:

```bash
python course/day-01/lab-03/lab02_safe_posture.py en6 --classic-walk
```

### Step 3 — Optional: interactive sport menu

**Run in your own terminal** (uses `input()` — will `EOFError` in non-interactive pipes):

```bash
./scripts/run_go2_highlevel.sh en6
```

Try `list` → **`balanced stand` (9)** if you skipped Step 2. Avoid **`0` damp** and flips.

### Step 4 — Cross-check with repo script

```bash
python scripts/go2_connection_check.py --interface en6 --skip-multicast
```

Lab 2 script and connection check should agree on PASS.

---

## 3. Exercises

### Exercise A — Topic vs RPC

Why is `SportClient.Init()` not enough to prove `rt/sportmodestate` is publishing?

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

`Init()` only registers the RPC client to the sport **service**. Topics use a separate DDS reader path; both are needed in practice.

</details>

### Exercise B — mcf vs ai

Why does this repo treat `mcf` as PASS on Go2 but G1 labs require `ai`?

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

Different robots and motion stacks; see [G1 vs Go2](../../../docs/G1-vs-GO2.md).

</details>

### Exercise C — Safety (written)

List three commands from the sport menu you must **not** run unsupervised in Day 1 (e.g. damp, flips) and why. When is `HandStand` allowed in this camp?

---

## 4. Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Action |
|---------|--------|
| `Readiness: FAIL` on DDS | Re-run [Lab 0](../lab-00/) |
| `code=0` but **no visible motion** | Run without `--skip-stand`; wait full **5 s** after StandUp; Unitree app **sport** mode; try menu `1` stand_up |
| PARTIAL `CheckMode` | Try Step 2 anyway in clear space |
| `EOFError` on Enter | Use a real terminal for `lab02_safe_posture.py` or menu |
| Robot frozen after damp | `BalanceStand` / `stand_up` / remote; see [GO2 Field Guide §5](../../../docs/GO2-FIELD-GUIDE.md) |

---

## 5. Deliverable

Submit:

- Log snippet: `lab02_sport_readonly.py en6` → `Readiness: PASS`  
- Log snippet: `lab02_safe_posture.py en6` → `Summary: PASS` + **what you saw** on the robot  
- Optional: `lab02_showcase.py en6` log + which gestures ran  
- Answer to Exercise A or C  

---

## 6. Next lab

**Next day:** [Day 2 — Obstacle avoidance](../../day-02/lab-02/) · [Day 2 — Inspection capture](../../day-02/lab-03/)

---

## References

- [`lab02_sport_readonly.py`](lab02_sport_readonly.py) · [`lab02_safe_posture.py`](lab02_safe_posture.py) · [`lab02_showcase.py`](lab02_showcase.py) · [`scripts/run_go2_highlevel.sh`](../../../scripts/run_go2_highlevel.sh)  
- [`docs/GO2-FIELD-GUIDE.md`](../../../docs/GO2-FIELD-GUIDE.md) · [G1 Lab 2 pattern](../../day-05/lab-02/)

code_files.name: lab02_safe_posture.py
code_files.code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Lab 2 — First visible SportClient motion: StandUp → BalanceStand → forward walk.

Default includes a short walk (most visible). Use --posture-only to skip translation.

Usage:
  python course/day-01/lab-03/lab02_safe_posture.py en6 --dry-run
  python course/day-01/lab-03/lab02_safe_posture.py en6
  python course/day-01/lab-03/lab02_safe_posture.py en6 --posture-only
  python course/day-01/lab-03/lab02_safe_posture.py en6 --vx 0.3 --move-sec 3
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import os
import sys
import time
from pathlib import Path

# Import shared helper from course/day-01/
_DAY01 = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
if str(_DAY01) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(_DAY01))

from go2_motion_helpers import (  # noqa: E402
    periodic_sport_move,
    set_gait_classic_walk,
    visible_stand_prep,
)

from unitree_sdk2py.core.channel import ChannelFactoryInitialize, ChannelSubscriber
from unitree_sdk2py.go2.sport.sport_client import SportClient
from unitree_sdk2py.idl.unitree_go.msg.dds_ import SportModeState_


def _init_dds(iface: str) -> None:
    if os.environ.get("CYCLONEDDS_URI"):
        print("Warning: CYCLONEDDS_URI is set; unset it for interface-only init.")
        ChannelFactoryInitialize(0)
    else:
        ChannelFactoryInitialize(0, iface)


def _wait_sportmodestate(timeout_s: float = 8.0) -> bool:
    got: list[int] = []

    def handler(msg: SportModeState_) -> None:
        got.append(1)

    sub = ChannelSubscriber("rt/sportmodestate", SportModeState_)
    sub.Init(handler, 10)
    deadline = time.time() + timeout_s
    while time.time() < deadline and not got:
        time.sleep(0.05)
    return bool(got)


def _confirm(walk: bool) -> bool:
    print()
    if walk:
        print("WARNING: Robot will STAND UP, balance, then walk forward ~3 s.")
    else:
        print("WARNING: Robot will STAND UP and balance (no forward walk).")
    print("  Clear ~2 m; spotter required.")
    try:
        return input("Type yes to continue: ").strip().lower() in ("yes", "y")
    except EOFError:
        print("No TTY — use a real terminal or --dry-run")
        return False


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    parser.add_argument("interface", nargs="?", default=os.environ.get("GO2_INTERFACE", "enp0s31f6"))
    parser.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--posture-only",
        action="store_true",
        help="StandUp + BalanceStand only (no forward walk)",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--skip-stand", action="store_true", help="Skip StandUp/BalanceStand prep")
    parser.add_argument("--vx", type=float, default=0.3, help="Forward m/s (default 0.3)")
    parser.add_argument("--move-sec", type=float, default=3.0, help="Walk duration (default 3 s)")
    parser.add_argument("--move-hz", type=float, default=20.0)
    parser.add_argument("--stand-up-wait", type=float, default=5.0, help="Seconds after StandUp")
    parser.add_argument("-y", action="store_true", help="Skip confirmation")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--classic-walk",
        action="store_true",
        help="Call ClassicWalk(True) after BalanceStand (sport gait RPC)",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    walk = not args.posture_only

    if not os.environ.get("CYCLONEDDS_HOME"):
        print("ERROR: CYCLONEDDS_HOME is not set.")
        return 1

    print(f"Interface: {args.interface}")
    plan = []
    if not args.skip_stand:
        plan += ["StandUp()", "BalanceStand()"]
    if walk:
        plan.append(f"Move({args.vx},0,0) ~{args.move_sec}s @ {args.move_hz}Hz")
        plan.append("StopMove()")

    if args.dry_run:
        print("[dry-run]", " → ".join(plan) if plan else "(nothing)")
        return 0

    if not args.y and not _confirm(walk):
        print("Aborted.")
        return 1

    _init_dds(args.interface)
    if not _wait_sportmodestate():
        print("FAIL: no sportmodestate — complete Lab 1 first")
        return 1

    sport = SportClient()
    sport.SetTimeout(10.0)
    sport.Init()

    try:
        visible_stand_prep(
            sport,
            skip=args.skip_stand,
            stand_up_wait_s=args.stand_up_wait,
        )

        if args.classic_walk:
            set_gait_classic_walk(sport, True)

        if walk:
            print(f"[walk] Move({args.vx}, 0, 0) for {args.move_sec}s")
            err = periodic_sport_move(
                sport, args.vx, 0.0, 0.0, args.move_sec, args.move_hz
            )
            if err != 0:
                print(f"    Move returned code={err}")
            print("[walk] StopMove()")
            print(f"        code={sport.StopMove()}")
        else:
            print("[walk] skipped (--posture-only)")

    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        sport.StopMove()
        return 130

    print()
    print("Summary: PASS — visible SportClient motion complete")
    print("  If you saw no movement: Unitree app sport mode, estop, or use menu stand_up (1).")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
